name: graphql-type
summary: GraphQL type definition with SDL and resolver mappings, registered with the schema.
description: Creates a GraphQL type definition and registers it in an initializer
problem: You're building a GraphQL API and need to add a type to the schema — its fields, relationships, and how each field resolves to data — in a way that lives alongside the rest of the application's code.
appliesWhen: Your application exposes a GraphQL API
You're adding a new domain type or extending an existing schema
You want type definitions to live with the code that backs them
Resolvers will pull from datastores, services, or other domain code
avoidWhen: Your API is REST-only — use phpnomad/controller instead
You're not using PHPNomad's GraphQL integration (the registration won't apply)
The shape only matters internally — use phpnomad/model
examples: Define an Order type for the GraphQL schema
Add a Subscription type to the API
tradeoffs: Couples the resolver to the type definition file — convenient for small types, awkward when resolvers grow. Refactor large types into separate resolver classes when needed.
outputs: A {name} type definition class with SDL string and resolver mappings
A registration adding it to the initializer's getTypeDefinitions() list
postApply: Define the type's fields in the SDL string
Implement resolver methods for each non-trivial field
Verify the schema compiles by booting the GraphQL endpoint

typeName.aiHint: PascalCase, no suffix. The name as it appears in the GraphQL schema (Order, User).

initializer.description: FQCN of the initializer to register in
initializer.aiHint: Pick the initializer that owns related GraphQL contributions.
